svg.text: impls & decorators all share this seam
all ops O(1) — no scan, no shift ✓
the class the interviewer wants to see
get → read lock · put / evict → write lock
thread-safety lives HERE — core stays lock-free
get() upgrades a read to a write to reorder the DLL
get(): expired ⇒ evict + report as MISS
adds time-to-live without touching the core
add behavior by composition, not subclassing
stores its KEY — so on evict we know
the DLL node — the whole trick lives here
updated by the core on every hit / miss / evict
which victim to drop when full
the Strategy knob — swap the victim rule

g.text: real node lives BETWEEN them, so

svg.text: THE O(1) MECHANISM — HashMap + doubly-linked list (inside LRUCacheImpl)
HashMap<K, CacheNode> — key ⇒ node in O(1)
pure pointer splice — no array shifting
HashMap = O(1) find · DLL = O(1) reorder/evict
neither alone is enough — you need BOTH
HashMap alone: no order · DLL alone: O(n) find

g.text: Mention it, THEN hand-roll HashMap+DLL to prove
you know the O(1) internals — that's what's tested.

svg.text: ① the LRUCache interface (seam) → ② LRUCacheImpl = HashMap + DLL,
all O(1) → draw the mechanism (sentinels, moveToHead, removeTail) → ③ layer Concurrent / TTL as Decorators over the same seam.
Patterns: Strategy (eviction) · Decorator (Concurrent / TTL / logging) · Doubly-linked list
LRU Cache — LLD (O(1) get/put via HashMap + doubly-linked list)
